title: Explicit formulae for Stark Units and Hilbert's 12th problem
speaker: Samit Dasgupta
abstract: Hilbert's 12th problem is to provide explicit analytic formulae for elements generating the maximal abelian extension of a given number field. In this talk I will describe an approach to Hilbert’s 12th that involves proving exact p-adic formulae for Gross-Stark units. This builds on prior joint work with Kakde and Ventullo in which we proved Gross’s conjectural leading term formula for Deligne-Ribet p-adic L-functions at s=0. This is joint work with Mahesh Kakde.

proof_stages.label: Hilbert twelfth problem and Stark-theoretic setting
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames explicit class field theory through the known cases of Q and imaginary quadratic fields, identifies totally real fields as the target case, and introduces Stark/Brumer-Stark conjectures as a route to explicit generators of abelian extensions.

proof_stages.label: Gross p-adic leading-term conjecture
proof_stages.summary: For a CM abelian extension H/F and an odd character, Deligne-Ribet p-adic L-functions are recalled and Gross's conjecture is stated: the order of vanishing at s=0 is controlled by split primes above p and the normalized leading term is a p-adic regulator of Brumer-Gross-Stark units.

proof_stages.label: Cohomological Ribet-method proof template
proof_stages.summary: Known results on the order and leading term are described as using Kummer-theoretic Galois cohomology, Ribet-style congruences between Eisenstein series and cusp forms, and the derivative of the U_p operator to recover the required p-adic logarithmic invariants.

proof_stages.label: Brumer-Stark units and the loss in regulators
proof_stages.summary: The relevant p-units are defined by their Stickelberger-valued valuations, Brumer-Stark integrality is stated, and the speaker explains why Gross's regulator gives only norms/determinants rather than explicit units, motivating an exact formula.

proof_stages.label: Eisenstein-cocycle formula and class-field generation
proof_stages.summary: An Eisenstein cocycle, Spieß's class, and a generator of a totally positive p-unit homology group are combined by cap products to give a conjectural p-adic integral formula for Brumer-Stark units, which computationally produces Hilbert class fields and would generate the CM abelian extensions needed for Hilbert's twelfth problem.

proof_stages.label: Integral Gross tower-fields congruences as a replacement for constructing units
proof_stages.summary: To avoid directly proving Brumer-Stark integrality, the speaker formulates an unconditional weak Gross tower-fields congruence in relative augmentation quotients, explains how auxiliary Taylor-Wiles primes and Chebotarev control the local components, and states that proving these congruences for all auxiliary data would imply the Eisenstein-cocycle exact formula, with higher-rank analogues expected to yield principal-minor formulas.

proof_stages.label: Group-ring Hilbert modular forms proof strategy
proof_stages.summary: The proposed proof constructs group-ring-valued Hilbert modular forms whose specializations vary the nebentypus, uses Eisenstein series with Stickelberger elements as constant terms and a cancellation combination to produce an Eisenstein congruence modulo I^2, then reads the theta_s/theta_r ratio from U_p and relates it via Hida/Ribet Galois representations to the desired cohomological congruence.
